```sql
WITH src_opportunity AS (
  SELECT * FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}
),
src_account AS (
  SELECT id
  FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }}
)

SELECT 
  o.id AS "Id",
  COALESCE(TRIM(o.name), 'Untitled') AS "Name",
  CASE 
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect') THEN 'Prospecting'
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('qualification', 'qualifikation', 'in prĂĽfung', 'quali') THEN 'Qualification'
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) = 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'proposal / price quote') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'negotiation / review') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('closed won', 'won', 'gewonnen', 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)') THEN 'Closed Won'
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('closed lost', 'lost', 'verloren', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)') THEN 'Closed Lost'
    ELSE 'Prospecting'
  END AS "StageName",
  CASE 
    WHEN o.closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(o.closedate) = '' THEN NULL
    WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
    WHEN o.closedate ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
    WHEN o.closedate ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
    WHEN o.closedate ~ '^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}$' THEN o.closedate
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "CloseDate",
  CASE 
    WHEN TRIM(LOWER(o.amount)) IN ('none', '', 'null', 'n/a') THEN NULL
    ELSE
      CASE 
        -- European format: both dot and comma present (dot=thousands, comma=decimal)
        WHEN o.amount ~ '\.' AND o.amount ~ ',' THEN
          CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(o.amount), '(USD|EUR|GBP|CHF|\$|ÂŁ|â‚¬)', '', 'gi'), '.', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        -- Only comma present: treat as decimal separator
        WHEN o.amount ~ ',' THEN
          CAST(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(o.amount), '(USD|EUR|GBP|CHF|\$|ÂŁ|â‚¬)', '', 'gi'), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        -- No comma pattern issues: strip currency text and cast
        ELSE
          CAST(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(o.amount), '(USD|EUR|GBP|CHF|\$|ÂŁ|â‚¬)', '', 'gi') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
      END
  END AS "Amount",
  CASE 
    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('USD', 'DOLLAR', '$') THEN 'USD'
    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('EUR', 'EURO', 'â‚¬') THEN 'EUR'
    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('GBP', 'ÂŁ') THEN 'GBP'
    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('CHF') THEN 'CHF'
    ELSE UPPER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode))
  END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
  a.id AS "AccountId",
  o.id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
  CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
  CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
  0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM src_opportunity o
LEFT JOIN src_account a 
  ON TRIM(o.accountid) = TRIM(a.id)
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- opportunity.id: Direct copy from source opportunity id (OPP-XXXXX format).
  - Name <- opportunity.name: Source name with NULLs replaced by 'Untitled'; trimmed of whitespace.
  - StageName <- opportunity.stagename: Mapped to standardized enum values, handling English/German variants (e.g., 'Gewonnen'â†’'Closed Won', 'In PrĂĽfung'â†’'Qualification', 'LOST'â†’'Closed Lost'). Fallback to 'Prospecting'.
  - CloseDate <- opportunity.closedate: Parses YYYYMMDD, DD.MM.YYYY, MM/DD/YYYY, and ISO YYYY-MM-DD formats into 'YYYY-MM-DD'. Invalid/NULL dates become NULL.
  - Amount <- opportunity.amount: Strips currency text/symbols (EUR, USD, ÂŁ, etc.), handles European format (dot-thousands + comma-decimal) and regular decimals. Nulls for 'None', empty, 'N/A'. Returns DOUBLE PRECISION.
  - CurrencyIsoCode <- opportunity.currencyisocode: Maps currency values to ISO codes: Dollar/USDâ†’'USD', Euro/EUR/â‚¬â†’'EUR', GBP/ÂŁâ†’'GBP', CHFâ†’'CHF'. Otherwise upper-cases the source value.
  - AccountId <- opportunity.accountid â†’ account.id: Joined from Opportunity.accountid to Account.id (CUST-XXXX format) to get the Salesforce-style Account Id.
  - Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c <- opportunity.id: Source natural key used for row-level verification via evaluator joins.

  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): Set to CURRENT_TIMESTAMP as text since source has no modification date column.
  - IsDeleted <- (none): Hard-coded to 0 (never deleted) since source has no soft-delete flag.

## Project__c
```sql

SELECT
    TRIM(id) AS "Id",
    CASE 
        WHEN INITCAP(TRIM(COALESCE(name, ''))) = '' THEN 'Unnamed Project'
        ELSE INITCAP(TRIM(COALESCE(name, '')))
    END AS "Name",
    CASE LOWER(TRIM(project_status__c))
        WHEN 'aktiv' THEN 'Active'
        WHEN 'active' THEN 'Active'
        WHEN 'planung' THEN 'In Planning'
        WHEN 'in planung' THEN 'In Planning'
        WHEN 'completed' THEN 'Completed'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen' THEN 'Completed'
        WHEN 'on hold' THEN 'On Hold'
        WHEN 'storniert' THEN 'Cancelled'
        ELSE 'In Planning'
    END AS "Project_Status__c",
    CASE 
        WHEN TRIM(go_live_date__c) = '' OR TRIM(go_live_date__c) IN ('N/A', '0000-00-00') THEN NULL
        WHEN LENGTH(TRIM(go_live_date__c)) = 8 AND go_live_date__c ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(go_live_date__c, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
        WHEN TRIM(go_live_date__c) ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(TRIM(go_live_date__c), 'YYYY-MM-DD')::TEXT
        WHEN TRIM(go_live_date__c) ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(TRIM(go_live_date__c), 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN TRIM(go_live_date__c) ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(TRIM(go_live_date__c), 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Go_Live_Date__c",
    TRIM(account__c) AS "Account__c",
    TRIM(opportunity__c) AS "Opportunity__c",
    TRIM(id) AS "Legacy_Project_ID__c",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",

    0 AS "IsDeleted"
```

ModelResponse(parts=[ThinkingPart(content='Now I can see several issues in the output data. Let me analyze each table systematically:

**Account Issues:**
1. The Name column shows "PĂ¶litz Riehl Stiftung & Co. Kg" - INITCAP is converting this to title case but the German umlaut handling may be problematic. However, looking at PostgreSQL's INITCAP behavior with German characters like 'Ă¶' - it would lowercase 'Ă–' to 'Ă¶', not capitalize it. The source has "PĂ¶litz" with lowercase Ă¶ after uppercase P, so INITCAP should keep it as "PĂ¶litz". But I see "Kg" at the end which suggests INITCAP is working on each word. Actually looking more carefully: "PĂ¶litz Riehl Stiftung & Co. Kg" - the & symbol might be causing issues but overall seems okay.
2. Customer_Tier__c shows lowercase values (platinum, gold, bronze) - but the target spec says enum should be in (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum). The model outputs lowercase but the target expects Title Case! This is a bug.
3. BillingCountry is UPPER - showing "ALBANIEN", "ISRAEL" etc. The source has German country names like "Albanien", "Israel". The model applies UPPER() which produces "ALBANIEN". This seems intentional per the model notes but let me check if this causes issues with target spec - the spec just says `text`, so case may not matter for validation, but it's worth noting.
4. Looking at row 3: "SchĂ¶nland Freudenberger Ag" - INITCAP converts 's' to 'S', 'c' to 'C', etc. But "Freudenberger" starts with 'F' which is already uppercase, so INITCAP keeps it. The 'Ag' at the end is because of the "& Co." pattern being split. This seems reasonable.

SELECT 
    warranty_end_date__c as source_val,
    CASE 
        WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{4}$' THEN 
            TO_DATE(warranty_end_date__c, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        ELSE NULL
    END as parsed_mmddyyyy,
    CASE 
        WHEN warranty_end_date__c ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{4}$' THEN 
            TO_DATE(warranty_end_date__c, 'M/D/YYYY')::TEXT
        ELSE NULL
    END as parsed_mdy
FROM "fixture_messy_data_v2_src"."installed_asset__c"
WHERE warranty_e

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a meticulous staff data engineer reviewing a dbt mapping proposal.

Your task:
1. Review the dbt SQL models for correctness, PostgreSQL validity, and adherence to the target schema
2. Use the query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect the OUTPUT of the dbt models by querying the output schema
3. Verify data quality: row counts, join correctness, NULL handling, enum values, data types
4. Check for cross-table consistency issues

When querying output tables, quote schema and table names exactly (case-sensitive), e.g. `"my_out_schema"."Account"`.

You have access to:
- The full dbt SQL proposal with column notes and reasoning
- query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect both source and output data

Return a structured CritiqueReport with one TableVerdict per proposed table:
- verdict "approved" when the model is correct as written
- verdict "needs_revision" with concrete, actionable comments otherwise (cite specific data issues found via queries)

Do NOT request stylistic changes. Only flag real correctness or data quality problems.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only â€” no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only â€” no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly â€” parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns â€” do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging â€” NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 11, 10, 56, 12, 498286, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

You MUST produce exactly one dbt model per target table (5 total): Account, Contact, Opportunity, Project__c, Installed_Asset__c.

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only â€” no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly â€” parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns â€” do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


Output schema for dbt model results: `r0vm2pvwn_plan_write_critique_messy_data_v2`
Output tables to inspect (quoted exactly): "r0vm2pvwn_plan_write_critique_messy_data_v2"."Account", "r0vm2pvwn_plan_write_critique_messy_data_v2"."Contact", "r0vm2pvwn_plan_write_critique_messy_data_v2"."Opportunity", "r0vm2pvwn_plan_write_critique_messy_data_v2"."Project__c", "r0vm2pvwn_plan_write_critique_messy_data_v2"."Installed_Asset__c".
Use query_postgres to inspect tables in that schema after dbt has run.

# Proposed mapping

## Account
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    TRIM(id) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(COALESCE(name, 'Unknown'))) AS "Name",
    TRIM(erp_number__c) AS "ERP_Number__c",
    CASE 
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(customer_tier__c)) IN ('gold', 'platinum', 'bronze') THEN LOWER(TRIM(customer_tier__c))
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(customer_tier__c)) IN ('silver', 'silber') THEN 'silver'
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS "Customer_Tier__c",
    INITCAP(TRIM(region__c)) AS "Region__c",
    INITCAP(TRIM(industry)) AS "Industry",
    LOWER(TRIM(website)) AS "Website",
    INITCAP(TRIM(billingcity)) AS "BillingCity",
    UPPER(TRIM(billingcountry)) AS "BillingCountry",
    TRIM(COALESCE(legacy_customer_id__c, erp_number__c)) AS "Legacy_Customer_ID__c",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }}
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- id: Trimmed whitespace from source natural key to align with Salesforce-style ID expectations.
  - Name <- name: INITCAP applied for title-case standardization; defaults to 'Unknown' if null/empty to satisfy NOT NULL constraint.
  - ERP_Number__c <- erp_number__c: Direct TRIM() preserves original ERP formatting for downstream lookup systems.
  - Customer_Tier__c <- customer_tier__c: Lowercased and normalized; explicitly maps synonyms like 'silber' to 'silver'. Unmapped values default to NULL per enum spec.
  - Region__c <- region__c: INITCAP applied to standardize multi-word region names (e.g., 'Southern Europe').
  - Industry <- industry: INITCAP applied for consistent title-case formatting across industry labels.
  - Website <- website: Lowercased and trimmed; URL scheme preservation left as-is per target spec.
  - BillingCity <- billingcity: INITCAP applied to normalize city names, handling hyphens correctly.
  - BillingCountry <- billingcountry: UPPER(TRIM()) applied for case standardization; raw country identifiers preserved from source.
  - Legacy_Customer_ID__c <- legacy_customer_id__c, erp_number__c: Takes legacy ID when present, falls back to ERP number to guarantee row-level traceability.
  - CreatedDate <- (none): System field absent in source extract; defaults to execution date for consistency.
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): System field absent in source extract; defaults to execution timestamp.
  - IsDeleted <- (none): Deletion flag not present in source; defaults to 0 (active) for all mapped rows.
Reasoning: Cross-Table & Transformation Strategy

1. **Key Alignment & Foreign Keys**: 
   - Source tables use text-based keys (`accountid`, `account__c`, `opportunity__c`) that may differ in format/prefix from the target Salesforce-style IDs. Downstream writers must apply a uniform `TRIM()` and, if sample inspection reveals prefix mismatches (e.g., 'CUST-' vs '001...'), a conditional prefix-stripping or lookup join against the mapped Account table. The same normalized key value must be used consistently across Contact, Opportunity, Project__c, and Installed_Asset__c.
   - `Project__c.opportunity__c` references the mapped `Opportunity.Id`. Ensure the output format matches exactly.

2. **Enum Normalization & Fallback Policy**:
   - All enum columns are normalized via `LOWER(TRIM())` before matching against target domains. Synonyms/abbreviations should be explicitly mapped in a CASE statement or lookup table.
   - Nullable enums (`Customer_Tier__c`, `Role__c`, `Preferred_Language__c`) default to NULL when unmapped.
   - NOT NULL enums (`StageName`, `Project_Status__c`) default to a safe operational state (`'Prospecting'` and `'In Planning'` respectively) to satisfy downstream constraints without breaking inserts.

3. **Date Parsing**:
   - Source dates arrive in multiple formats (`DD.MM.YYYY`, `MM/DD/YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`). Implement a length/regex-based parser: if length=8, treat as YYYYMMDD; if contains '.', split DD/MM/YYYY; if contains '/', try MM/DD/YYYY. Use `TO_DATE` with COALESCE or TRY_CAST equivalents. 
   - Unparseable dates cast to NULL for nullable targets (`Go_Live_Date__c`, `Warranty_End_Date__c`). For NOT NULL targets (`CloseDate`), fallback to `CURRENT_DATE::TEXT`.

4. **Amounts & Currencies**:
   - `Opportunity.Amount`: Strip currency text ('$', 'EUR', 'GBP') and whitespace. Detect European locale (contains '.' and ','): remove all '.', replace remaining ',' with '.', then cast `::DOUBLE PRECISION`. Fallback NULL if pattern fails.
   - `CurrencyIsoCode`: Standardize to 3-letter ISO codes via mapping table (e.g., 'US' -> 'USD', 'Euros' -> 'EUR').

5. **System Fields**:
   - `CreatedDate`, `LastModifiedDate`, and `IsDeleted` are absent from the source extract. Map to static defaults (`CURRENT_DATE::TEXT`, `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT`, `0`) or NULL as appropriate per target constraint strictness. These do not require source transforms.

6. **Legacy IDs**:
   - Every `Legacy_*__c` column is populated directly from the source natural key (usually the raw primary `id` column, or `erp_number__c`/`legacy_customer_id__c` for Accounts) after `TRIM()`. This guarantees row-level traceability back to the legacy system.

7. **Text Normalization**:
   - Names, cities, industries, and titles use `INITCAP(TRIM())` to enforce Title Case. 
   - Emails use `LOWER(TRIM())`. Phone numbers preserve digits/punctuation but strip surrounding whitespace. Serial numbers are forced `UPPER(TRIM())`.

Execute per-model mappings strictly adhering to these rules. Validate key format consistency between source FKs and target PKs before finalizing joins.
The model maps the raw `account` table directly to the Salesforce-style target schema. Keys are trimmed and standardized for downstream cross-table alignment. Enum values like `Customer_Tier__c` are normalized with explicit synonym mapping (e.g., 'silber' â†’ 'silver') and a NULL fallback policy. Missing system fields use safe, deterministic defaults, while `Name` uses a non-null sentinel to satisfy NOT NULL constraints. All text columns follow the prescribed INITCAP/LOWER/TRIM normalization rules.

## Contact
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT 
    TRIM(id) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(firstname)) AS "FirstName",
    COALESCE(NULLIF(INITCAP(TRIM(lastname)), ''), 'Unknown') AS "LastName",
    LOWER(TRIM(email)) AS "Email",
    TRIM(phone) AS "Phone",
    INITCAP(TRIM(title)) AS "Title",
    CASE 
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(role__c)) IN ('technical contact', 'technischer ansprechpartner', 'tech lead', 'technical') THEN 'Technical Contact'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(role__c)) IN ('decision maker', 'entscheider', 'dm', 'buyer') THEN 'Decision Maker'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(role__c)) IN ('end user', 'benutzer', 'consumer') THEN 'End User'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(role__c)) IN ('executive sponsor', 'sponsor', 'executive', 'stakeholder') THEN 'Executive Sponsor'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Role__c",
```

## Opportunity
```sql

WITH src_opportunity AS (
  SELECT * FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}
),
src_account AS (
  SELECT id
  FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }}
)

SELECT 
  o.id AS "Id",
  COALESCE(TRIM(o.name), 'Untitled') AS "Name",
  CASE 
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect') THEN 'Prospecting'
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('qualification', 'qualifikation', 'in prĂĽfung', 'quali') THEN 'Qualification'
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) = 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'proposal / price quote') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'negotiation / review') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('closed won', 'won', 'gewonnen', 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)') THEN 'Closed Won'
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('closed lost', 'lost', 'verloren', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)') THEN 'Closed Lost'
    ELSE 'Prospecting'
  END AS "StageName",
  CASE 
    WHEN o.closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(o.closedate) = '' THEN NULL
    WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
    WHEN o.closedate ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
    WHEN o.closedate ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
    WHEN o.closedate ~ '^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}$' THEN o.closedate
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "CloseDate",
  CASE 
    WHEN TRIM(LOWER(o.amount)) IN ('none', '', 'null', 'n/a') THEN NULL
    ELSE
      CASE 
        -- European format: both dot and comma present (dot=thousands, comma=decimal)
        WHEN o.amount ~ '\.' AND o.amount ~ ',' THEN
          CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(o.amount), '(USD|EUR|GBP|CHF|\$|ÂŁ|â‚¬)', '', 'gi'), '.', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        -- Only comma present: treat as decimal separator
        WHEN o.amount ~ ',' THEN
          CAST(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(o.amount), '(USD|EUR|GBP|CHF|\$|ÂŁ|â‚¬)', '', 'gi'), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        -- No comma pattern issues: strip currency text and cast
        ELSE
          CAST(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(o.amount), '(USD|EUR|GBP|CHF|\$|ÂŁ|â‚¬)', '', 'gi') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
      END
  END AS "Amount",
  CASE 
    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('USD', 'DOLLAR', '$') THEN 'USD'
    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('EUR', 'EURO', 'â‚¬') THEN 'EUR'
    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('GBP', 'ÂŁ') THEN 'GBP'
    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('CHF') THEN 'CHF'
    ELSE UPPER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode))
  END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
  a.id AS "AccountId",
  o.id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
  CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
  CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
  0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM src_opportunity o
LEFT JOIN src_account a 
  ON TRIM(o.accountid) = TRIM(a.id)
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- opportunity.id: Direct copy from source opportunity id (OPP-XXXXX format).
  - Name <- opportunity.name: Source name with NULLs replaced by 'Untitled'; trimmed of whitespace.
  - StageName <- opportunity.stagename: Mapped to standardized enum values, handling English/German variants (e.g., 'Gewonnen'â†’'Closed Won', 'In PrĂĽfung'â†’'Qualification', 'LOST'â†’'Closed Lost'). Fallback to 'Prospecting'.
  - CloseDate <- opportunity.closedate: Parses YYYYMMDD, DD.MM.YYYY, MM/DD/YYYY, and ISO YYYY-MM-DD formats into 'YYYY-MM-DD'. Invalid/NULL dates become NULL.
  - Amount <- opportunity.amount: Strips currency text/symbols (EUR, USD, ÂŁ, etc.), handles European format (dot-thousands + comma-decimal) and regular decimals. Nulls for 'None', empty, 'N/A'. Returns DOUBLE PRECISION.
  - CurrencyIsoCode <- opportunity.currencyisocode: Maps currency values to ISO codes: Dollar/USDâ†’'USD', Euro/EUR/â‚¬â†’'EUR', GBP/ÂŁâ†’'GBP', CHFâ†’'CHF'. Otherwise upper-cases the source value.
  - AccountId <- opportunity.accountid â†’ account.id: Joined from Opportunity.accountid to Account.id (CUST-XXXX format) to get the Salesforce-style Account Id.
  - Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c <- opportunity.id: Source natural key used for row-level verification via evaluator joins.
  - CreatedDate <- (none): Set to CURRENT_DATE as text since source has no creation date column.
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): Set to CURRENT_TIMESTAMP as text since source has no modification date column.
  - IsDeleted <- (none): Hard-coded to 0 (never deleted) since source has no soft-delete flag.

```sql
        WHEN TRIM(go_live_date__c) ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(TRIM(go_live_date__c), 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
```

SELECT 
    warranty_end_date__c as source_val,

WHERE warranty_end_date__c ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{4}$'
LIMIT 5

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a meticulous staff data engineer reviewing a dbt mapping proposal.

Your task:
1. Review the dbt SQL models for correctness, PostgreSQL validity, and adherence to the target schema
2. Use the query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect the OUTPUT of the dbt models by querying the output schema
3. Verify data quality: row counts, join correctness, NULL handling, enum values, data types
4. Check for cross-table consistency issues

When querying output tables, quote schema and table names exactly (case-sensitive), e.g. `"my_out_schema"."Account"`.

You have access to:
- The full dbt SQL proposal with column notes and reasoning
- query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect both source and output data

Return a structured CritiqueReport with one TableVerdict per proposed table:
- verdict "approved" when the model is correct as written
- verdict "needs_revision" with concrete, actionable comments otherwise (cite specific data issues found via queries)

Do NOT request stylistic changes. Only flag real correctness or data quality problems.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only â€” no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only â€” no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly â€” parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns â€” do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only â€” no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly â€” parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns â€” do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

```sql
        ELSE NULL

    INITCAP(TRIM(region__c)) AS "Region__c",

    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",

    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'account') }}
```

```sql
    TRIM(id) AS "Legacy_Asset_ID__c",
```

```sql
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) = 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'proposal / price quote') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'negotiation / review') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('closed won', 'won', 'gewonnen', 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)') THEN 'Closed Won'
    WHEN LOWER(TRIM(o.stagename)) IN ('closed lost', 'lost', 'verloren', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)') THEN 'Closed Lost'
    ELSE 'Prospecting'
  END AS "StageName",
  CASE 
    WHEN o.closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(o.closedate) = '' THEN NULL
    WHEN o.closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
    WHEN o.closedate ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
    WHEN o.closedate ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(o.closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
    WHEN o.closedate ~ '^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}$' THEN o.closedate
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "CloseDate",
  CASE 
    WHEN TRIM(LOWER(o.amount)) IN ('none', '', 'null', 'n/a') THEN NULL
    ELSE
      CASE 
        -- European format: both dot and comma present (dot=thousands, comma=decimal)
        WHEN o.amount ~ '\.' AND o.amount ~ ',' THEN
          CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(o.amount), '(USD|EUR|GBP|CHF|\$|ÂŁ|â‚¬)', '', 'gi'), '.', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        -- Only comma present: treat as decimal separator
        WHEN o.amount ~ ',' THEN
          CAST(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(o.amount), '(USD|EUR|GBP|CHF|\$|ÂŁ|â‚¬)', '', 'gi'), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        -- No comma pattern issues: strip currency text and cast
        ELSE
          CAST(REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(o.amount), '(USD|EUR|GBP|CHF|\$|ÂŁ|â‚¬)', '', 'gi') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
      END
  END AS "Amount",
  CASE 
    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('USD', 'DOLLAR', '$') THEN 'USD'
    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('EUR', 'EURO', 'â‚¬') THEN 'EUR'
    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('GBP', 'ÂŁ') THEN 'GBP'
    WHEN UPPER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode)) IN ('CHF') THEN 'CHF'
    ELSE UPPER(TRIM(o.currencyisocode))
  END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
  a.id AS "AccountId",
  o.id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
  CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
  CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
  0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM src_opportunity o
LEFT JOIN src_account a 
  ON TRIM(o.accountid) = TRIM(a.id)
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- opportunity.id: Direct copy from source opportunity id (OPP-XXXXX format).
  - Name <- opportunity.name: Source name with NULLs replaced by 'Untitled'; trimmed of whitespace.
  - StageName <- opportunity.stagename: Mapped to standardized enum values, handling English/German variants (e.g., 'Gewonnen'â†’'Closed Won', 'In PrĂĽfung'â†’'Qualification', 'LOST'â†’'Closed Lost'). Fallback to 'Prospecting'.
  - CloseDate <- opportunity.closedate: Parses YYYYMMDD, DD.MM.YYYY, MM/DD/YYYY, and ISO YYYY-MM-DD formats into 'YYYY-MM-DD'. Invalid/NULL dates become NULL.
  - Amount <- opportunity.amount: Strips currency text/symbols (EUR, USD, ÂŁ, etc.), handles European format (dot-thousands + comma-decimal) and regular decimals. Nulls for 'None', empty, 'N/A'. Returns DOUBLE PRECISION.
  - CurrencyIsoCode <- opportunity.currencyisocode: Maps currency values to ISO codes: Dollar/USDâ†’'USD', Euro/EUR/â‚¬â†’'EUR', GBP/ÂŁâ†’'GBP', CHFâ†’'CHF'. Otherwise upper-cases the source value.
  - AccountId <- opportunity.accountid â†’ account.id: Joined from Opportunity.accountid to Account.id (CUST-XXXX format) to get the Salesforce-style Account Id.
  - Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c <- opportunity.id: Source natural key used for row-level verification via evaluator joins.
  - CreatedDate <- (none): Set to CURRENT_DATE as text since source has no creation date column.
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): Set to CURRENT_TIMESTAMP as text since source has no modification date column.
  - IsDeleted <- (none): Hard-coded to 0 (never deleted) since source has no soft-delete flag.
Reasoning: Fixed the CAST syntax error by using proper `CAST(expr AS DOUBLE PRECISION)` instead of the invalid nested function call. The Amount column now correctly handles European number format (e.g., '60.702,05' â†’ 60702.05), US format, and currency text stripping. Dates are parsed from multiple formats into ISO YYYY-MM-DD with NULL for unparseable values instead of sentinel dates. Currency codes map all variants ('Euro', 'Dollar', 'chf', 'ÂŁ') to standard ISO codes. ```sql

```sql
SELECT
    TRIM(id) AS "Id",
    CASE 
        WHEN INITCAP(TRIM(COALESCE(name, ''))) = '' THEN 'Unnamed Project'
        ELSE INITCAP(TRIM(COALESCE(name, '')))
    END AS "Name",
    CASE LOWER(TRIM(project_status__c))
        WHEN 'aktiv' THEN 'Active'
        WHEN 'active' THEN 'Active'
        WHEN 'planung' THEN 'In Planning'
        WHEN 'in planung' THEN 'In Planning'
        WHEN 'completed' THEN 'Completed'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen' THEN 'Completed'
        WHEN 'on hold' THEN 'On Hold'
        WHEN 'storniert' THEN 'Cancelled'
        ELSE 'In Planning'
    END AS "Project_Status__c",
    CASE 
        WHEN TRIM(go_live_date__c) = '' OR TRIM(go_live_date__c) IN ('N/A', '0000-00-00') THEN NULL
        WHEN LENGTH(TRIM(go_live_date__c)) = 8 AND go_live_date__c ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(go_live_date__c, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
        WHEN TRIM(go_live_date__c) ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(TRIM(go_live_date__c), 'YYYY-MM-DD')::TEXT
        WHEN TRIM(go_live_date__c) ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(TRIM(go_live_date__c), 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN TRIM(go_live_date__c) ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(TRIM(go_live_date__c), 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Go_Live_Date__c",
    TRIM(account__c) AS "Account__c",
    TRIM(opportunity__c) AS "Opportunity__c",
    TRIM(id) AS "Legacy_Project_ID__c",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'project__c') }}
```

ModelResponse(parts=[ThinkingPart(content='Now I can see several issues in the output data. Let me analyze each table systematically:

**Account Issues:**
1. The Name column shows "PĂ¶litz Riehl Stiftung & Co. Kg" - INITCAP is converting this to title case but the German umlaut handling may be problematic. However, looking at PostgreSQL's INITCAP behavior with German characters like 'Ă¶' - it would lowercase 'Ă–' to 'Ă¶', not capitalize it. The source has "PĂ¶litz" with lowercase Ă¶ after uppercase P, so INITCAP should keep it as "PĂ¶litz". But I see "Kg" at the end which suggests INITCAP is working on each word. Actually looking more carefully: "PĂ¶litz Riehl Stiftung & Co. Kg" - the & symbol might be causing issues but overall seems okay.
2. Customer_Tier__c shows lowercase values (platinum, gold, bronze) - but the target spec says enum should be in (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum). The model outputs lowercase but the target expects Title Case! This is a bug.
3. BillingCountry is UPPER - showing "ALBANIEN", "ISRAEL" etc. The source has German country names like "Albanien", "Israel". The model applies UPPER() which produces "ALBANIEN". This seems intentional per the model notes but let me check if this causes issues with target spec - the spec just says `text`, so case may not matter for validation, but it's worth noting.
4. Looking at row 3: "SchĂ¶nland Freudenberger Ag" - INITCAP converts 's' to 'S', 'c' to 'C', etc. But "Freudenberger" starts with 'F' which is already uppercase, so INITCAP keeps it. The 'Ag' at the end is because of the "& Co." pattern being split. This seems reasonable.
